perturbation.original_text: All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information and all copies thereof which are in the possession of DISCLOSEE shall be and remain the property of NUSD and shall be promptly returned or destroyed upon NUSD’s request. Documents prepared by DISCLOSEE using Confidential Information, or derived therefrom, shall be destroyed upon request of NUSD. Written confirmation of destruction of Confidential Information as requested by NUSD shall be provided by DISCLOSEE to NUSD.
perturbation.changed_text: All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information and all copies thereof which are in the possession of DISCLOSEE shall be and remain the property of NUSD and *may* be promptly returned or destroyed upon NUSD’s request. Documents prepared by DISCLOSEE using Confidential Information, or derived therefrom, *may* be destroyed upon request of NUSD. Written confirmation of destruction of Confidential Information *if* requested by NUSD *must* be provided by DISCLOSEE to NUSD.
perturbation.explanation: The original text enforces the return or destruction of materials containing confidential information. The modification makes the return/destruction of documents optional by using "may". By changing "shall be destroyed upon request" to "*may* be destroyed upon request", creates a discrepancy and gives uncertainty on when a destruction is enforced or not. The last modification enforces "Written confirmation of destruction of Confidential Information", to "Written confirmation of destruction of Confidential Information *if* requested by NUSD *must* be provided by DISCLOSEE to NUSD.", to enforce it if requested, but since is is now optional, can add to the uncertainty and can contradict itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Return and Destruction of Materials. All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information and all copies thereof which are in the possession of DISCLOSEE shall be and remain the property of NUSD and *may* be promptly returned or destroyed upon NUSD’s request. Documents prepared by DISCLOSEE using Confidential Information, or derived therefrom, *may* be destroyed upon request of NUSD. Written confirmation of destruction of Confidential Information *if* requested by NUSD *must* be provided by DISCLOSEE to NUSD.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'shall' to 'may' regarding the return or destruction of confidential information, making it optional. This directly contradicts the original intent and creates uncertainty about the DISCLOSEE's obligations. The contradicted text shows the same change was made, therefore reinforcing the contradiction by the introduction of uncertainty regarding the return or destruction of confidential information, and when the written confirmation is needed, which would cause confusion.

perturbation.original_text: (c) All Confidential Information and Materials are and shall remain the property of NUSD. By disclosing information to DISCLOSEE, NUSD does not grant any express or implied right to DISCLOSEE to or under NUSD Confidential Information and Materials. Further, the delivery and disclosure of Confidential Information and Materials does not constitute a License permitting DISCLOSEE to use said Information and/or Materials for any purpose other than that allowed by NUSD as specified above.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) All Confidential Information and Materials are and shall remain the property of NUSD, *unless specified*. By disclosing information to DISCLOSEE, NUSD does not grant any express or implied right to DISCLOSEE to or under NUSD Confidential Information and Materials, *unless permitted*. Further, the delivery and disclosure of Confidential Information and Materials *constitutes a general License* permitting DISCLOSEE to use said Information and/or Materials for *any* purpose allowed by NUSD as specified above.
perturbation.explanation: In the original text it is enforced that "All Confidential Information and Materials are and shall remain the property of NUSD", but with the introduced change, we weaken this, making it conditional, and creating an in-text contradiction. The addition of "unless specified" allows for exceptions, contradicting the initial statement that all information remains NUSD's property. In the original text, the document explicitly states that “NUSD does not grant any express or implied right to DISCLOSEE to or under NUSD Confidential Information”. Adding "unless permitted" to this phrase makes the clause ambiguous and contradictory and provides exceptions. The contradiction is amplified as now “delivery and disclosure of Confidential Information and Materials constitutes a general License permitting DISCLOSEE to use said Information and/or Materials for *any* purpose allowed by NUSD as specified above.”
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Miscellaneous (b) NUSD and the DISCLOSEE are jointly responsible for ensuring the protection of personally identifiable student information in compliance with the Family Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA). The DISCLOSEE acknowledges that it shall not take any action that may cause the DISCLOSEE or NUSD to be out of compliance with FERPA, California Education Code 49073.1, or NUSD Board Policy 5125. (c) All Confidential Information and Materials are and shall remain the property of NUSD, *unless specified*. By disclosing information to DISCLOSEE, NUSD does not grant any express or implied right to DISCLOSEE to or under NUSD Confidential Information and Materials, *unless permitted*. Further, the delivery and disclosure of Confidential Information and Materials *constitutes a general License* permitting DISCLOSEE to use said Information and/or Materials for *any* purpose allowed by NUSD as specified above. (d) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to subject matter hereof and merges all prior discussions between them as Confidential Information. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement date and signed by both parties. (e) None of the provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of NUSD, its agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorized officer of NUSD. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any other provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion. (f) If either NUSD or DISCLOSEE employs attorneys to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys’ fees. This Agreement shall be constructed and controlled by the laws of the State of California, and DISCLOSEE further consents to jurisdiction by the state and federal courts sitting in Sacramento County in the State of California. Process may be served on either party by U.S. Mail, postage prepaid, certified or registered return receipt requested. (g) Subject to the limitations set forth in this Agreement, this Agreement will inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties, their successors and assigns. (h) If any provisions of this Agreement shall be held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect. (i) All obligations created by this Agreement shall survive change or termination of the parties’ business relationship.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the contract. The addition of 'unless specified' and 'unless permitted', coupled with 'constitutes a general license permitting DISCLOSEE to use said Information and/or Materials for *any* purpose allowed by NUSD', directly undermines the contradicted text, which emphasizes NUSD's ownership and restrictions on DISCLOSEE's rights. This creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.
